title: UCSD DSC 106 Project 1: Relationship Visualization of Price Per Calorie and Processing Level of Food Products
description: More processed foods generally cost less per calorie, especially at Walmart and Target where the decline across NOVA classes is clear. WholeFoods shows a weaker and less consistent version of this trend.

title: UCSD DSC 106 Project 2: Persuasive or Deceptive Visualization? NYPD Complaint Analysis
description: Analyzed NYPD CCRB complaint data to show how the same evidence can support different narratives. Compared a persuasive view and a more cautious uncertainty-aware view to examine how visual design choices shape interpretation.

title: NTU Research Project: Teacher Emotion and Student Outcomes (PISA 2022)
description: Used the PISA 2022 dataset to study how teacher emotion relates to student academic performance and other standardized indicators. Applied Hierarchical Linear Modeling (HLM) to analyze relationships between teacher-level and student-level variables.

title: Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM), Problem C
description: Participated in the 2026 MCM/ICM, working on Problem C. Used Bayesian and machine learning methods to build models and analyze the problem, contributing to model development, data analysis, and technical report writing.
